{"docName":"about_zhi_du_guan.txt","chunkIndex":4,"text":"Three things make this approach work. First, embedding search is fast:\neven a few thousand passages compare in milliseconds. Second, modern\nsmall language models — three to eight billion parameters — give\nsurprisingly good answers when grounded in real source text. Third,\nkeeping everything on-device removes the recurring cloud cost and\nsidesteps the privacy concerns of uploading your reading {"docName":"rag_concepts.md","chunkIndex":9,"text":"The second pitfall is chunk size. Too small, and a single sentence\nloses its context. Too large, and you waste model tokens on irrelevant\ntext.
